actual_run_inference: CTRL-1: the falsifier described by this control is armed in this run.

expected_signature.expected_value: drift well outside the reconstruction tolerance, or amplitude explosion at scaled beta
expected_signature.pass_rule: wrong scaling produces a clearly out-of-tolerance signature

baseline_hypothesis.plain_statement: Naive / wrong operator scaling of the gauge action should fail or visibly diverge under the reconstruction metric.
baseline_hypothesis.why_this_matters: If wrong scaling does not fail, the reconstruction metric cannot distinguish correct from incorrect gauge action — meaning every other Program 1 result is unreliable.

candidate_lemmas.name: Operator-Action Separation Lemma (instrument health)
candidate_lemmas.statement: On this run's window, Naive / wrong operator scaling of the gauge action should fail or visibly diverge under the reconstruction metric held within tolerance. Formalizing this as a finite/proxy lemma is the next research step.
what_it_does_not_prove: this supports Program 1's positive witnesses directly
this proves NC2 in any formal sense

raw_observations.verifier_interpretation: Control armed: naive gamma scaling diverges from baseline by 452.9% (max_drift=67.93 vs baseline_amplitude=15).
